notes: KNOWN FAILING (5/7) — final_turn_end + teardown fail: aider 0.86.2 does NOT reliably emit the FINAL turn's working→ready settle before tmux teardown. The load-bearing phases all pass: session_birth, escape_start (working), escape_end (working→ready — the /run shell-escape does NOT stick in working, the scenario's actual primitive), pid_bind, and final_turn_start (the session is still alive and a real prompt drives working). The failing tail is aider's idle-driven turn-end (IdleFlush ~1500ms gated behind the SessionDetector ~5s staleWorkingRefreshInterval): when the final turn's last transcript write coincides with the working transition (no subsequent fswatcher activity to re-arm the tailer), the refresh-ticker settle races process death and is lost — reproduced across 4 recordings with trailing sleeps 12/18/20s and both edit and non-edit final prompts. This is the assessed irrlicht_observes:partial latency carve-out, NOT a daemon regression and NOT recipe-fixable (cf. pi/turn-aborted-by-error teardown). Clears if the daemon settles a quiet working session on process_exit (emit working→ready before the exit transition) or shortens the final-turn idle-flush latency. Original rationale follows. DOCUMENTED TARGET — authored from the aider transport + the claudecode/codex/pi siblings; not yet validated against a live recording (aider has no provider auth in this environment, so recording is deferred until auth is available). PRIMITIVE: aider's in-chat `/run <cmd>` (documented alias `!<cmd>`) runs a shell command locally with no LLM round-trip — the direct one-to-one analog of Claude Code's `!cmd` shell-escape this scenario is modeled on (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html). The user-observable signal asserted here is the safety arc: ready→working→ready with the session NOT left stuck in working after the command exits. OBSERVES:PARTIAL CARVE-OUT (the whole reason this cell is partial, NOT yes): irrlicht observes the `/run` escape ONLY as a state arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event. OBSERVABILITY CORRECTION (confirmed against the live aider 0.86.2 recording, supersedes the earlier doc-based `> Running`-fold prediction): aider 0.86.2 does NOT write a `> Running <cmd>` blockquote for `/run`. It writes a `> Add command output to the chat?` confirmation prompt followed by `> Added N lines of output to the chat.` — neither matches the parser's runningRE (^>\s*Running\s+, parser.go:69-70), so p.toolCall("Bash") (parser.go:122-124) NEVER fires and ZERO Bash tool_results are emitted for the escape. The earlier assessment predicted a `> Running` → Bash tool_result fold (same path as `> Applied edit`, parser.go:119); that prediction is WRONG for 0.86.2 — there is no tool_result at all. What still holds: the `#### /run echo hello` line opens a turn (→working) and ~1500ms later IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done (working→ready), so the ready→working→ready arc is intact and never sticks. CONSEQUENCE: this spec asserts the ARC (a real working→ready cycle that does not stick) plus a final normal turn proving the session is alive, and asserts NO tool_result/Bash marker. The claudecode-shaped verify.tool_calls_max:0 in scenarios.json happens to hold for aider 0.86.2 in fact (zero tool calls), but for a DIFFERENT reason than claudecode (which skips its <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> wrappers): aider's `/run` simply never produces a parseable tool line. The phase DSL has no tool_calls assertion form anyway; the no-tool_result finding is documented scope (observability_correction), not asserted as a phase. TRANSPORT: aider is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; there is no transcript-UUID rollout session — the whole arc lives on the one proc-<PID> session row keyed by the daemon's process scanner (cf. basic-turn / agent-question-pending specs). MECHANICS: `/run echo hello` writes a `#### /run echo hello` user-prompt line (the `####` opens a turn → working), then aider's `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output` confirmation blockquotes (NOT a `> Running` line, so no Bash tool_result — see OBSERVES:PARTIAL above), then nothing more until ~1500ms quiet → IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done (working→ready), clamped behind the SessionDetector's ~5s working-refresh poll (the assessed observes:partial latency carve-out — a delay, not a correctness gap, so the arc is still asserted). RECORDED IN INTERACTIVE MODE (drive-aider-interactive.sh: tmux REPL), NOT headless `aider --message`: headless exits at turn end and the final ready settle is lost to a process_exited race; the interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across the escape and the final turn, torn down only at script end via tmux kill-session (no Ctrl-D / /exit). SESSION-IDENTITY WORKAROUND (same as agent-question-pending and the spec/SKILL.md proc-<PID> pitfall): the live REPL may surface a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session; escape_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never goes working), and pid_bind / escape_end / final_turn_start / final_turn_end / teardown pin to escape_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW RUNS DIFFER: the shell command's stdout, exact final-turn assistant wording, per-turn token counts, PID, session UUID, and sub-second timing all vary. Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready escape arc that does NOT stick in working, a second ready→working→ready arc for the normal prompt, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch

The /run echo hello shell-escape opens a turn → working (binds the bindable session; a transient pre-session never enters working). Aider runs the command locally and writes a `> Add command output to the chat?` / `> Added N lines of output` confirmation pair — NOT a `> Running` line — so the parser's runningRE never fires and NO Bash tool_result is emitted (the observes:partial scope, documented; the escape is observed only as the state arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event). No tool_result/Bash marker is asserted here

PID bound to the live aider session at REPL birth (aider binds the proc-<PID> via the process scanner / command-line match ~ms after launch, BEFORE the first prompt), so anchor to recording start, not escape_start — same shape as aider/basic-turn and interrupted-turn. Still pinned same_session_as the bindable escape session so a co-occurring pre-session can't satisfy it

After the shell command exits, IdleFlush settles the turn working→ready — the escape does NOT stick in working and never enters waiting. This is the load-bearing user-observable signal of the scenario
A normal user prompt drives ready→working — proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives the arc after the shell-escape
The final turn completes back to ready; state never entered waiting
The driver tears down the REPL (tmux kill-session) → a single process_exited on the session
